label: Packaging
summary: Entrypoints, package layout, and import-surface hygiene.

rules.label: Cross Package Internal Import
rules.description: Local Python packages reaching into another package's internal or private modules.
rules.explanation: In Python, Cross Package Internal Import matters because the pattern here, Local Python packages reaching into another package's internal or private modules, makes ownership and navigation harder when someone has to trace behavior across modules or services.
rules.fix: For Cross Package Internal Import in Python, move the code to the package, module, router, or entrypoint layer that already owns this boundary so navigation and responsibility stay aligned.

rules.label: Pyproject Missing Requires Python
rules.description: pyproject metadata missing an explicit Python runtime requirement.
rules.explanation: In Python, Pyproject Missing Requires Python matters because the pattern here, pyproject metadata missing an explicit Python runtime requirement, is worth review because import surfaces and entrypoints shape how predictable the repository feels to users and maintainers.
rules.fix: For Pyproject Missing Requires Python in Python, keep package roles obvious, avoid surprising side effects on import, and put entrypoint code where tooling and humans expect it.

rules.label: Pyproject Script Entrypoint Unresolved
rules.description: pyproject script entrypoints that do not resolve to a locally indexed module callable.
rules.explanation: In Python, Pyproject Script Entrypoint Unresolved matters because the pattern here, pyproject script entrypoints that do not resolve to a locally indexed module callable, makes ownership and navigation harder when someone has to trace behavior across modules or services.
rules.fix: For Pyproject Script Entrypoint Unresolved in Python, move the code to the package, module, router, or entrypoint layer that already owns this boundary so navigation and responsibility stay aligned.

rules.label: Python Public API Any Contract
rules.description: Public Python APIs that expose Any in parameter or return contracts.
rules.explanation: In Python, Python Public API Any Contract matters because the pattern here, Public Python APIs that expose Any in parameter or return contracts, is worth review because import surfaces and entrypoints shape how predictable the repository feels to users and maintainers.
rules.fix: For Python Public API Any Contract in Python, keep package roles obvious, avoid surprising side effects on import, and put entrypoint code where tooling and humans expect it.
